commit.message:
Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

commit.message:
Add pivot-aware scanning to network-recon, improve dashboard agent lifecycle

network-recon skill:
- Add "Pivot Mode" section with two-phase scanning approach
- Phase 1: fast host discovery on pivot host (ARP, ping sweep, DNS zone,
  single-port proxychains sweep)
- Phase 2: targeted nmap through proxychains on known-live hosts only
- Document nmap MCP server limitation (no proxychains support)
- Add static nmap alternative for speed-critical scenarios
- Add pivot-specific troubleshooting entries

Dashboard (tail-agent.py):
- Auto-discover new agents from tasks directory (no .dashboard file needed)
- Red *stopped* indicator in pane header for dead agents
- Detect agent completion via JSONL final message + 5s mtime debounce
- Detect killed agents (TaskStop) via 30s mtime staleness
- Keep stopped agents visible instead of auto-removing
- Space bar toggles agents in/out from browser overlay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add network pivoting support: tunnels table, pivoting-agent, interim writes

- Add tunnels table to state.db (schema v2→v3) with tunnel_type,
  pivot_host, target_subnet, local_endpoint, requires_proxychains, status
- Add tunnel MCP tools: get_tunnels (all modes), add_tunnel (interim+writer),
  update_tunnel (writer only); include Tunnels section in get_state_summary()
- Remove CHECK constraint from state_events.event_type to allow 'tunnel' events
- Create pivoting-agent (sonnet, skill-router + shell-server + state-interim)
  for tunnel setup via pivoting-tunneling skill with sudo handoff protocol
- Update all 5 discovery skills to use state-interim MCP with interim writes
  (add_credential, add_vuln, add_pivot, add_blocked) for real-time findings
- Add pivoting workflow to orchestrator: pivot-to-tunnel flow, tunnel context
  in agent prompts, tunnel health checks, pivoting-agent routing
- Update docs: CLAUDE.md, state-server README, orchestrator routing tables

commit.message:
V1 pricing fallback: use parent room rates for linked/dependent rooms

When a room has 0 V1 rate rules, check raw_data dependencies for
includeBookingsRoomId1 (Beds24's parent room link). If found, use
the parent room's rate rules for pricing. No extra API call needed —
getRates already returns all rooms in the property.

Fixes rooms 550515-550517 (Reine des mers child rooms) which inherit
pricing from parent room 550667 (Villa entière).

shows 'direct') with actionable external source analytics: client
type bars (AI agents highlighted green), top external user agents
table, and external endpoint access breakdown. Adds external_top_
user_agents and external_by_endpoint queries in storage + server.

commit.message:
feat: 加入规则过滤层（硬过滤 + 软重排序）

- 删除"内容风格"输入（运营同事难以判断）
- 新增 4 个筛选维度：品牌定位类型、最低CTR要求、品类经验要求、新品冷启动模式
- 硬过滤：CTR阈值 / 品类经验占比，不达标达人直接剔除
- 软重排序：按品牌定位调整最终分（模型分65%+信号35%）
  - 成分型 → CTOR加权
  - 生活方式型 → CTR加权
  - 底妆/视觉型 → 互动率加权
  - 套装性价比型 → RPM加权
- 新品冷启动 → 额外互动率加权（70%+30%）
- 推荐理由根据品牌定位类型自动切换展示维度
- 结果页显示过滤摘要（移除人数、重排序信号名称）
- recommend() 现返回全部候选，top_n截断移至规则层之后

commit.message:
Add V2 AI sales chat agent (Cole Gordon 7 Beliefs framework)

V2 is a complete rewrite of the sales chat prompt and knowledge base, built on
Cole Gordon's Closers Into Leaders methodology. Runs alongside V1 behind a
feature flag (?chatv=v4 or CHAT_PROMPT_VERSION=v4 env var).

V2 KB: 8-phase conversation flow, 4 knowledge base files (~87k tokens),
7 annotated example conversations, conditional objection handling.

Key features: selective mirroring, mid-pitch deferral, LTV-based ROI estimation
with visitor self-proof, depth probing for short answers, "I don't know" handling.

Code changes: V2 prompt loading in chat route, nudge timer 60s->120s with
clear-on-typing, Slack cost estimation now branches on V1/V2 token count,
save route maxDuration increased to 60s for longer V2 conversations.

commit.message:
research: add multi-agent decision-making and deliberation workflow candidates

Appended §11 subsection "意思決定・議論ワークフロー候補 (リサーチエージェント調査, 2026-03-05)"
covering 8 concrete workflow/template candidates (POST /workflows/debate, ADR, Delphi,
Red/Blue, Socratic, /deliberate command, heterogeneous_debate config, DECISION.md format)
grounded in: Du et al. ICML 2024, Chan et al. ChatEval ICLR 2024, A-HMAD Springer 2025,
DEBATE ACL 2024, CONSENSAGENT ACL 2025, SocraSynth arXiv 2402.06634, RT-AID ScienceDirect 2025,
and MAD Requirements Engineering arXiv 2507.05981.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: ingest NOVAS_REGRAS.MD — 19 new business rules from directorate

Cross-referenced 86 rules from satellite/docs/NOVAS_REGRAS.MD against
the 54 existing RAG business rules. Inserted 19 genuinely new rules
covering previously undocumented behaviors:

- Card hierarchy type constraints (4-level strict validation)
- Cascade deletion (Epic → Story → Task → Subtask → recursive)
- Card activation triggers child generation (15-20/5-8/3-5)
- Project context locking on first Epic activation (irreversible)
- Project lifecycle states (draft → processing → active)
- Interview fixed questions + context generation rules
- Workflow state machines per item type (Epic/Story/Task/Bug/Subtask)
- Bug severity field (exclusive to Bug type)
- Job priority levels with specific assignments (10/7/5/3)
- Prompt queue scoring (5 factors + manual override)
- RAG continuous scan ordering (SCHEMA→ROUTES→LOGIC→PRESENTATION→CONFIG)
- AI Flow chain: one active chain per usage_type
- Per-model concurrency and timeout configuration
- Card traceability (batch_source, interview_insights, status_transitions)
- Card relationships (blocks, depends_on, relates_to, duplicates, clones)
- Specs scope (framework=global, project=scoped)
- Wiki rule pages parent constraint (regras-de-negocio)
- Contracts as source of truth for system behavior (70 YAML contracts)
- Codebase memory scan: 4 phases + file priority order

Total business rules: 54 → 73

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(sdk): implement Go SDK v1.0.0

Complete Go client library for the ZKP middleware service.

Packages:
- github.com/ssanjeewa/go-sdk — HTTP client + typed errors + input validation
- github.com/ssanjeewa/go-sdk/crypto — standalone ECIES + secp256k1 + BN254 field

Core features:
- ZKPClient with all 10 API methods (Health, Metrics, IssueCredential,
  IssueCredentialBatch, GenerateProof, PrepareShare, IncomingShares,
  GetUserPubKey, ResolveEmail, SetAPIKey)
- Typed error hierarchy: AuthError, RateLimitError, ValidationError,
  NetworkError, NotFoundError, ServerError, TreeFullError, CryptoError
- Exponential backoff retry (min(base*2^n, 30s) ± 10% jitter) for
  5xx / 429 / transport errors; Retry-After header honoured
- Per-attempt context timeout via context.WithTimeout
- SetAPIKey: atomic.Pointer[string] — concurrent-safe key rotation
- Client-side BN254 field validation for n/s before HTTP call (SEC-03)

Crypto sub-package:
- GenerateKeyPair / PrivateKeyToPublicKey / IsValidPublicKey
- EncryptSecret / DecryptSecret — ECIES wire format identical to
  zkp-middleware/internal/crypto/ecies.go and @zkp-system/node-sdk
- BN254ScalarField, IsValidFieldElement, ParseFieldElement

Tests: 83 tests across both packages, all passing with -race detector
Examples: issue_credential, generate_proof, share_file
CI: .github/workflows/go-sdk.yml (Go 1.22, tidy+vet+test+golangci-lint)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 2-terminal pot zero-effect bug in PassiveRType stages

Pots in MNA-based passive stages were stamped as fixed resistors and
never created as DynNode::Pot, so control binding fell through to
PreGain and position changes had no audio effect. Now pots are stamped
in the G matrix at their initial resistance AND stored as DynNode::Pot
children for binding. On pot change the MNA G matrix is updated
(unstamp old conductance, stamp new) and the scattering matrix is
re-derived, throttled to every 32 samples like multi-NL stages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: V5 combined backtest + honest audit + V4 daily/spread strategies

V5 Combined (backtest_v5_combined.py):
- 10 pattern detectors (consec_pb, inside_break, ema_pullback, etc.)
- Multi-symbol backtest (RB+I) with 15min resampling
- EMA20 trend filter, next-bar-open entry, SL-first priority
- Three-factor decomposition: EMA(62%) + bar_position(20%) + pattern(18%)

V5 Audit (backtest_v5_audit.py):
- Equal notional weighting (RB 3 lots vs I 2 lots)
- Walk-forward OOS validation (train 2009-2019, test 2020-2025)
- Corrected random baseline (3 methods, Z=2.98 significant)

V4 Daily (backtest_v4_daily.py):
- V4a-V4e: ML daily prediction + 5min execution strategies
- LightGBM with 12 causal daily features

V4 ORB+Spread (backtest_v4_orb_spread.py):
- V4f: Opening Range Breakout
- V4g: RB-I spread arbitrage (Z-score mean reversion)

Infrastructure fixes:
- state_machine: 9 exit modes (fixed/atr/trail/choch/swing/BES etc.)
- smc_detector: causal swing delay parameter for all downstream functions
- smc_features: causal=True default
- adaptive_strategy: fix look-ahead bias in adaptive weights
- backtest_smc/multi: MTF mode parameter (daily_prev/ema5m/none)

fix: invalidate stale exe-patch backup after upgrade-claude

After installing a new Claude Code binary, remove the old .claude-proxy.bak
backup and patch history entries. Without this, the exe-patch system would
read the old backup as the patch source and overwrite the newly installed
binary with a patched copy of the old version.

- Add invalidateExePatchState() to clean up backup file and patch history
- Add TestInvalidateExePatchState to verify cleanup
- Bump version to v0.0.39

commit.message:
refactor: pre-defined navigation map replaces AI-generated Playwright steps

ARCHITECTURE CHANGE:
- OLD: Vision AI invented Playwright steps on every call (unreliable, slow)
- NEW: Vision only classifies screenshot (section + step, ~200 tokens)
       Screenshotter executes pre-defined steps from navigation-map.json

navigation-map.json:
- 25 sections with urlPath, waitFor selector, named step presets
- Feed: default, pinned_messages, favorites_filter, create_post, more_menu
- CRM_Deals: default, kanban_view, list_view, create_deal, settings
- Tasks: default, my_tasks, create_task, gantt
- Tariff_Widget: default, limits (clickFirst with fallback selectors)
- All other sections: default + section-specific steps

vision.js:
- New simplified prompt: classify section + step (200 tokens max vs 700)
- Returns {section, step, description, path, urlPath}
- Validates section/step against map, graceful fallback to Feed/default
- parseClaudeJSON() with 5 fallback strategies (kept from previous fix)
- max_tokens 700→200 (faster, cheaper)

screenshotter.js:
- Load navigation-map.json at startup
- New executeNavigation(page, base, section, step) using map
- executeNavAction() handles: waitTimeout, waitForSelector, clickFirst, click, fill, scroll
- clickFirst tries selectors in order until one is visible
- Legacy executeSteps() kept but no longer used in main flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: 진단 로그 복사/공유 버튼 추가

- 복사 버튼: 클립보드에 전체 로그 텍스트 복사 (1.5초 후 '복사됨!' 피드백)
- 공유 버튼: iOS 공유시트 → 메모/메일/카카오톡 등으로 전송 가능

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace passlib with bcrypt directly to fix startup crash

passlib 1.7.4 (unmaintained since 2020) uses a >72-byte internal test
string in its bcrypt bug-detection code, which now raises ValueError
with bcrypt >=4.1.0. Switching to bcrypt directly avoids this entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: embed risk data generation into create_sqlite_db.py for reliable Railway deploy

generate_risk_data.py was failing silently when run as a separate process
in the start command (errors caught by try/except, uvicorn still started but
with empty risk snapshot table).

Fix: import and call generate_risk_data.run() directly at the end of
create_sqlite_db.py so both run in the same process with guaranteed
identical DB path. Remove the separate python generate_risk_data.py &&
step from nixpacks.toml and railway.json start commands.

Any failure is now visible in Railway startup logs and the app still starts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: Complete Phase 3 - Add comprehensive PEP 257 docstrings to 45+ utility scripts

Enhanced docstrings for all utility, context management, and failure prevention
scripts across the 3-level architecture system:

Context Management (13 files):
- auto-context-pruner, context-cache, context-estimator, context-extractor
- context-monitor-v2, file-type-optimizer, monitor-and-cleanup-context
- monitor-context, smart-cleanup, smart-file-summarizer, tiered-cache

Session Management (8 files):

User Preferences (4 files):

Execution System (12 files):
- prompt-generator, task-phase-enforcer
- model-selection-enforcer, model-selection-monitor
- ast-code-navigator, auto-tool-wrapper
- pre-execution-optimizer, smart-read
- skill-auto-suggester, skill-manager
- check-incomplete-work
- auto-commit-detector, auto-commit-enforcer, auto-commit, trigger-auto-commit

Failure Prevention (6 files):
- failure-detector, failure-detector-v2, failure-learner
- failure-pattern-extractor, failure-solution-learner, pre-execution-checker

Changes:
- Added comprehensive module-level docstrings where missing
- Enhanced all class docstrings with Attributes sections
- Documented all method/function parameters with Args and Returns
- Added type hints and example usage where applicable
- Ensured 100% ASCII encoding (no Unicode characters)
- All files validated with python -m py_compile

Standard: PEP 257 Google-style docstrings
Coverage: 100% classes, 95%+ methods/functions
